messages.content: Where should I save my space-invaders cart?
Save it under `~/.motoi/carts/space-invaders/space-invaders.scm`. Every project gets its own folder inside `~/.motoi/carts/`, and the runnable cart file sits at the top of that folder.
I made a drum pattern for space-invaders. Where does the .grid file go?

Music source files live under `~/.motoi/carts/space-invaders/assets/music/`. Name it something like `~/.motoi/carts/space-invaders/assets/music/main-loop.grid` — the `.grid` is the sequencer input; the compiled `.scm` lands next to your cart.
Where do I put the sprite sheet for my space-invaders animation?
Sprite sheets go under `~/.motoi/carts/space-invaders/assets/animations/`. So the ship animation would be `~/.motoi/carts/space-invaders/assets/animations/ship.sheet`.
My space-invaders program saves a screenshot. Where should that file end up?

Generated artifacts belong in `~/.motoi/artifacts/space-invaders/` — one folder per artifact family. Screenshots, exports, cached AI outputs all land there. That way user work under `~/.motoi/carts/` stays tidy.
Where should I save my snake cart?

How do I organize a space-invaders game project?

Give space-invaders its own folder under `~/.motoi/carts/`:

```
~/.motoi/carts/space-invaders/
  space-invaders.scm                       ; the runnable cart
  assets/
    music/                       ; .grid files for the sequencer
    animations/                   ; .sheet files for the animator
```

Everything for space-invaders lives inside that one folder. Back it up and you've backed up the whole game.
I'm starting a new project called space-invaders. What folders do I need?
Start with `~/.motoi/carts/space-invaders/`. Add `assets/music/` and `assets/animations/` when you have sources for either. Nothing else is required — Motoi discovers the layout by convention.

Where does Motoi save cortex memory?
Cortex lives at `~/.motoi/cortex.slat` — one file, one user. `(cortex/remember ...)` writes there; `(cortex/recall ...)` reads.
Where are the tools that ship with Motoi?
The extras ship inside the repo under `tools/`. The two flagship tools are `tools/sequencer/` (music) and `tools/animator/` (sprites). Both emit pure Motoi Scheme.
Do my user carts go inside the Motoi git repo?
No — never write into the repo from a running cart. User work goes under `~/.motoi/carts/`. The repo is runtime; `~/.motoi/` is your own.
Where do the example carts that came with Motoi live?
Bundled examples live at `<repo>/carts/` (read-only, part of the git tree). Your own carts go under `~/.motoi/carts/<project>/`.
If I want to back up everything I've made in Motoi, what do I copy?
Copy `~/.motoi/` — one folder holds every cart, every asset, cortex memory, and every artifact. Nothing user-owned lives anywhere else.
What's in ~/.motoi/artifacts/?
Generated outputs — screenshots, exports, cached AI results, anything a cart produces. Organized as one subfolder per artifact family, e.g. `~/.motoi/artifacts/plots/`.
Where does the sequencer put its output file by default?
`~/.motoi/carts/<project>/<name>.scm`. Pass `--project foo --name my-beat` and the output lands at `~/.motoi/carts/foo/my-beat.scm`. Override with `--out <path>` for anywhere else.
Where does the animator put its output file by default?
Same convention as the sequencer: `~/.motoi/carts/<project>/<name>.scm`. The animator emits a Motoi cart that draws frame-by-frame.
Is there a global place for shared music between projects?
No — Motoi's convention is per-project assets. If you want to reuse a pattern, copy the `.grid` file into the new project's `assets/music/`. Small on purpose: each project stays self-contained.
Where do the sequencer's own examples live?
Inside the repo at `tools/sequencer/examples/`. They're read-only reference input; use them to learn the .grid format, then author your own under `~/.motoi/carts/<project>/assets/music/`.
Where do the animator's own examples live?
Inside the repo at `tools/animator/examples/`. Same story as the sequencer — study them, then author your own `.sheet` files under `~/.motoi/carts/<project>/assets/animations/`.
Can I delete ~/.motoi/artifacts/ if it gets big?
Yes — artifacts are ephemeral. Deleting `~/.motoi/artifacts/` never touches your carts or cortex. Carts recompute artifacts on demand.
Where do I put a helper .scm module I want to share between two of my carts?
Put shared code inside a project of its own: `~/.motoi/carts/shared/<name>.scm`. Each project stays a folder; a "library" project is just another folder.
If I share a cart with a friend, what do I send them?
The project folder: `~/.motoi/carts/<project>/`. They drop it into their own `~/.motoi/carts/` and run `motoi run ~/.motoi/carts/<project>/<name>.scm`.
Where does Motoi Copilot's cortex live vs. my own?
The Copilot's model weights are under the repo (or fetched). Your personal cortex — the facts you asked Motoi to remember — lives at `~/.motoi/cortex.slat`. Two different things, two different homes.
I have a song called title-theme for space-invaders. What's the full path?
`~/.motoi/carts/space-invaders/assets/music/title-theme.grid`. When you compile it, the output cart lands at `~/.motoi/carts/space-invaders/title-theme.scm`.
I have a sprite sheet called game-over for snake. What's the full path?
`~/.motoi/carts/snake/assets/animations/game-over.sheet`. When you compile it, the output cart lands at `~/.motoi/carts/snake/game-over.scm`.

How do I run the sequencer with default output?
`node tools/sequencer/sequencer.js from-grid path/to/pattern.grid --project my-game --name main-loop` writes to `~/.motoi/carts/my-game/main-loop.scm`.
How do I run the animator with an explicit output path?
`node tools/animator/animator.js from-sheet path/to/sprites.sheet --out ~/.motoi/carts/my-game/intro.scm`.
How do I try the sequencer without any input file?
`node tools/sequencer/sequencer.js demo --out /tmp/beat.scm` writes a four-on-the-floor sample. Play it: `bin/motoi run /tmp/beat.scm`.
How do I try the animator without any input file?
`node tools/animator/animator.js demo --out /tmp/anim.scm` writes a blinking-heart sample. Play it: `bin/motoi run /tmp/anim.scm`.
How do I run a Motoi cart from disk?
`bin/motoi run <path>.scm`. So a project cart is `bin/motoi run ~/.motoi/carts/my-game/my-game.scm`.
I want to preview a sequencer output before saving. What's the shortest way?

Compile to a scratch path, then run:

```
node tools/sequencer/sequencer.js from-grid my.grid --out /tmp/preview.scm
bin/motoi run /tmp/preview.scm
```

When it sounds right, re-emit with `--project <name> --name <name>` to land in `~/.motoi/carts/<name>/<name>.scm`.
Can the sequencer overwrite my saved cart?
Yes — passing the same `--project`/`--name` (or `--out`) rewrites the target file. Version-control or copy first if you want to keep the previous take.
How does the animator name output when I don't pass --name?
It falls back to the input file's basename. `from-sheet ship.sheet` without `--name` emits `~/.motoi/carts/default/ship.scm`.
Where do I put a .grid file so 'sequencer from-grid' can find it?
Anywhere readable by node — the tool takes a path. Convention: `~/.motoi/carts/<project>/assets/music/<name>.grid`. Then compile with `--project <project> --name <name>`.
What arg tells the sequencer which project a cart belongs to?
`--project <name>`. Combined with `--name`, this picks the output path: `~/.motoi/carts/<project>/<name>.scm`.
How do I check what the sequencer's TUI can do?
Launch it: `node tools/sequencer/sequencer.js tui --project my-game --name experiment`, then type `help` at the prompt.
How do I check what the animator's TUI can do?
Launch it: `node tools/animator/animator.js tui --project my-game --name experiment`, then type `help` at the prompt.
What if I want a cart to live outside ~/.motoi/?
Pass `--out <path>` — the tools honor any writable path. Default lives under `~/.motoi/carts/` so backups stay simple.
Where should test carts I don't want to keep go?
Use the repo's `scratch/` while iterating, e.g. `--out scratch/experiment.scm`. Promote to `~/.motoi/carts/<project>/` when the cart is worth keeping.
Where should I keep a private note about a cart, for my future self?
Alongside the cart: `~/.motoi/carts/<project>/NOTES.slat`. Since the project is a folder, sibling notes travel with it.
Confirm the sequencer's output shape is legal Motoi Scheme.
Yes — the sequencer emits calls like `(audio/tempo 120)` and `(define <track>-hits '(...))`. Running a slice returns `4`, confirming those forms parse and execute cleanly.
Show me a one-liner that plays a kick drum. Where would I save it?

```motoi
(synth/kit 'kick)
```

Save that as `~/.motoi/carts/hello-drum/hello-drum.scm` and run `bin/motoi run ~/.motoi/carts/hello-drum/hello-drum.scm`. Return value: `ok`.
Show me a one-liner that strikes middle C. Where would I save it?

```motoi
(note/strike 60 0.25)
```

Save as `~/.motoi/carts/hello-note/hello-note.scm`. Return value: `ok`.
Show me a one-liner that draws a red circle. Where does the cart go?

```motoi
(begin (set-mode 'default) (clear 0) (set-color 8) (circle 40 40 20) (framebuffer))
```

Save as `~/.motoi/carts/red-circle/red-circle.scm`. Return value: `ok`.
Show me a one-liner that pokes a single pixel. Where does the cart go?

```motoi
(begin (set-mode 'default) (pset 10 10 12))
```

Save as `~/.motoi/carts/one-pixel/one-pixel.scm`. Return value: `ok`.
Show me a one-liner that plays a C-major chord. Where does the cart go?

```motoi
(synth/chord (list 60 64 67) 0.5)
```

Save as `~/.motoi/carts/c-major/c-major.scm`. Return value: `ok`.
Show me a one-liner that fills the screen with color 12. Where does the cart go?

```motoi
(begin (set-mode 'default) (rect-fill 0 0 80 80))
```

Save as `~/.motoi/carts/blue-screen/blue-screen.scm`. Return value: `ok`.
Show me a one-liner that schedules a note 0.25s in. Where does the cart go?

```motoi
(note/place-at 60 0.25 0.25)
```

Save as `~/.motoi/carts/off-beat/off-beat.scm`. Return value: `ok`.
Prove that four kick hits and four snare hits together take the tempo I set.
`(audio/tempo 128)` sets BPM to 128; a subsequent `(audio/tempo)` reports `128`. A generated cart under `~/.motoi/carts/<project>/<name>.scm` uses this exact call to lock tempo.
Confirm the animator's per-frame render loop is legal Motoi Scheme.
Yes — that mirrors what the animator emits: mode → clear → draw → render. Result: `ok`. A generated cart lands at `~/.motoi/carts/<project>/<name>.scm`.
Show me a snippet that draws a line between two points.

```motoi
(begin (set-mode 'default) (clear 0) (set-color 11) (line 5 5 60 60))
```

Save under `~/.motoi/carts/<project>/line.scm`. Return value: `ok`.
Show me a snippet that plays a scale walking up.

```motoi
(begin (note/strike 60 0.1) (note/strike 62 0.1) (note/strike 64 0.1) (note/strike 65 0.1))
```

Save under `~/.motoi/carts/<project>/scale.scm`. Return value: `ok`.
Show me the shape of a two-track pattern the sequencer would emit.
That's the shape: two hit lists per track. Lengths: `(4 2)`. The compiled cart wraps these in `(sequence-track ...)` calls under `~/.motoi/carts/<project>/<name>.scm`.
Show me the shape of one animator frame.
That's one frame — clear, poke pixels, render (omitted here for eval). Result: `ok`. The animator wraps this per-frame under `~/.motoi/carts/<project>/<name>.scm`.
Prove that (note/strike) uses only CORE verbs.
Yes — `note/strike` is in the Motoi CORE registry. Return value: `ok`. That's why the sequencer emits it: kid-model retention.
Should I put my .grid file inside the Motoi git repo?
No — that's runtime territory. Your `.grid` sources belong under `~/.motoi/carts/<project>/assets/music/`. Only the repo maintainers write to the repo.
Can I have two projects share the same cart name?
Yes — the full path is `~/.motoi/carts/<project>/<name>.scm`, so `carts/snake/main.scm` and `carts/pong/main.scm` are separate files.
Should I put music sources in assets/animations/ if the folder is easier to type?
No — the convention matters. `.grid` files go under `assets/music/`, `.sheet` files under `assets/animations/`. Motoi Copilot and the tools discover work by folder name.
Can cortex.slat live inside a project folder?
No — cortex is per-user, not per-project. It lives at `~/.motoi/cortex.slat`. Memory belongs to you, not to any one game.
Is it OK to write generated screenshots into ~/.motoi/carts/<project>/?
No — user-authored files vs. generated artifacts are kept apart. Screenshots go under `~/.motoi/artifacts/<name>/` so you can wipe them without touching your cart.
Can I run the sequencer from the repo directory and have the .scm land in the repo?
Only if you pass `--out <repo-path>`. The default is `~/.motoi/carts/<project>/<name>.scm` — outside the repo — because your carts are yours.
Does the animator ever reach the network?
No — the animator is hermetic. It reads its input, writes a `.scm` file, and exits. No fetch, no exec. Priya's security review 2026-07-17 confirmed.
Does the sequencer ever reach the network?
No — same as the animator. Reads input, writes `.scm`, exits. No fetch, no exec.
Where do the sequencer + animator output binary assets?
They don't — both emit only text `.scm` files. If your cart wants to save a screenshot it does so at runtime under `~/.motoi/artifacts/<name>/`.
What if I need to store a cart in a git repo I own?
Perfectly fine — pass `--out /path/to/your-repo/game.scm`. `~/.motoi/` is the default, not a jail.
Can I nest projects under carts?
Convention: one project per folder, siblings under `~/.motoi/carts/`. Nesting works technically but breaks discovery. Prefer flat.
What lives at ~/.motoi/carts/?
One folder per project. Each folder holds the runnable `.scm`, an optional `assets/` subtree, and any project notes.
Can two carts share the same .grid source?
Yes — the sequencer takes an arbitrary input path. Convention still puts the `.grid` under one project's `assets/music/`; other carts reference it by full path when compiling.
Where does the file-org doctrine live in the repo?
`tools/FILE-ORG-DOCTRINE.slat` — locked 2026-07-17 by Su Jin + Priya. That file is the canonical layout.
Can I just make up a new folder like ~/.motoi/random/?
Nothing stops you, but Motoi's tools won't discover it. Stick to the layout in `tools/FILE-ORG-DOCTRINE.slat` so the Copilot can help you when you ask.
